mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor market demand, which raises expectations of higher future income and aggregate demand, thereby pushing up long-term interest rates as the market anticipates tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward yield changes is negative or insignificant (p>0.10), or more than 40% of signals produce opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below the 6.5% threshold (the Fed's Evans guidance anchor) triggers market repricing of the expected path of federal funds rate, lifting term premiums and causing the 10-year yield to rise.
failure_condition: After each trigger, the 5-day forward yield change is negative more than half the time, or the average yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate self-reinforcing demand momentum in both the residential and labor markets, which boosts long-term growth expectations and thus raises Treasury yields.
failure_condition: In periods when both proxies rise from their previous month, the 60-day forward yield change is negative more than one-third of the time, or the joint signal does not improve over the individual signals.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment where the fed funds rate is near zero, rising consumer price inflation erodes real yields and forces nominal yields upwards as investors demand compensation for reduced purchasing power.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, a 1% increase in CPIAUCSL over a month is followed by a fall in 20-day yield in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger factory output and capacity utilization, which leads to tighter resource markets and higher capital demand, pushing up real interest rates and nominal yields.
failure_condition: The rank IC of INDPRO changes versus 20-day yield changes is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) exceed a high threshold (e.g., 450,000 million dollars), it signals exceptionally strong consumer spending that raises inflation expectations and the growth outlook, lifting the 10-year yield.
failure_condition: Over the sample since 2000, after the trigger, the average 20-day yield change is not positive or the success rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero-lower-bound environment, the combination of a persistent near-zero fed funds rate and rising CPI inflation compresses real yields and forces investors to demand higher term premiums, causing the 10-year yield to rise.
failure_condition: In the ZLB regime, when both FEDFUNDS remains low and CPI rises, the 5-day yield change is negative more than 30% of the time or the joint signal has no predictive power over the single CPI signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is above 6%, strong monthly payroll growth signals that labor slack is being absorbed without triggering wage-push inflation concerns, so yields rise on stronger growth expectations but without the negative effect of anticipated rate hikes.
failure_condition: In the UNRATE > 6% regime, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day yield changes is not positive and significant at 95% confidence, or the signal is weaker than in the UNRATE ≤ 6% regime.
